facts.text: Morristown National Historical Park, America's first National Historical Park, preserves four Revolutionary War sites including Jockey Hollow and Washington’s Headquarters.
Speedwell Ironworks, a National Historic Landmark, hosted the first public demonstration of the electric telegraph on January 11, 1838.
The town’s name honors Lewis Morris, the first royal governor of New Jersey who championed land ownership rights for colonists.
The Academy of Saint Elizabeth, founded in Morristown in 1860, later found itself in Convent Station after 1895 boundary changes.
In 1950, the United States Equestrian Team was founded at the Coates estate on Van Beuren Road in Morristown.
The New York Herald dubbed Morristown the “Millionaire City of the Nation” in 1902 after noting 45 millionaires had built country homes there.
